candidates.name: Mario Diaz-Balart
candidates.bio: Mario Rafael Díaz-Balart y Caballero (/bəˈlɑːrt/ bə-LART; born September 25, 1961) is an American politician serving as the U.S. representative for Florida's 26th congressional district. A member of the Republican Party, he was elected in 2002, and his district includes much of northwestern Miami-Dade County, including Hialeah, and much of the northern portion of the Everglades.
key_messages: Díaz-Balart supports free trade agreements and was rated 75% by the National Foreign Trade Council for support of trade engagement. He voted for trade agreements with Peru, Chile, Singapore, Australia, and Central America.
Díaz-Balart supports repealing the Affordable Care Act and voted for the American Health Care Act in 2017.

Díaz-Balart has sought bipartisan immigration solutions and supported Trump's travel ban. In 2026, he cosponsored the DIGNIDAD Act proposing a pathway to legal status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants paired with stricter border enforcement.
Díaz-Balart opposes federal regulation of greenhouse gas emissions and is skeptical of global warming as a basis for Congressional action, fearing economic harm.
Díaz-Balart opposes requiring states to adopt federal education standards.
Díaz-Balart opposes gun-control legislation, stating after the Parkland shooting that gun control would not be effective at stopping mass shootings.
Díaz-Balart opposes mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenders.

Díaz-Balart maintains a strong stance on Cuban policy, supporting the embargo and opposing what he views as appeasement of the Castro regime. He also supported a 2025 appropriations bill banning UN funding as part of an 'America First foreign policy.'
Díaz-Balart opposes income tax increases and supports lowering taxes as a means of promoting economic growth. He voted for the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017.

platform_excerpts.excerpt: Díaz-Balart voted to promote free trade with Peru, against assisting workers who lose jobs due to globalization, for the Central America Free Trade Agreement, for the US-Australia Free Trade Agreement, for the US-Singapore free trade agreement, and for free trade with Chile.
On May 4, 2017, Díaz-Balart voted to repeal the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) and pass the American Health Care Act.

In 2026, Díaz-Balart was a cosponsor of the DIGNIDAD Act, which proposes a pathway to legal status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants, paired with stricter border enforcement and mandatory work and restitution requirements.

In 2007, Díaz-Balart said, 'My fear is using the bandwagon of global warming to have Congress act on some knee-jerk reaction which will please some editorialists, will hurt our economy, will not do anything to help us in the future.'
opposes requiring states to adopt federal education standards
Díaz-Balart said gun control legislation would not be effective at stopping mass shootings, saying, 'I want to make sure we look at things that could make a difference.'
opposes mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenders

Díaz-Balart supported a July 2025 appropriations bill that would ban funding for the United Nations secretariat and UNRWA, saying that the bill 'strengthens national security and supports an America First foreign policy.'

opposes an income tax increase, opposes mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenders, opposes federal spending as a means of promoting economic growth, supports lowering taxes as a means of promoting economic growth

top_stances.summary: Repeal the Affordable Care Act and replace with market-based solutions.
top_stances.track_record_note: Voted YEA on American Health Care Act in 2017, directly aligning with stance on ACA repeal.

top_stances.summary: Support free trade agreements and tax cuts for economic growth.
top_stances.track_record_note: Voted YEA on Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017; rated 75% by National Foreign Trade Council for trade support.

top_stances.summary: Lower taxes and oppose income tax increases to promote growth.
top_stances.track_record_note: Voted YEA on Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017, directly aligning with stated opposition to tax increases.

top_stances.summary: Strong anti-Cuba stance, embargo support, America First foreign policy.
top_stances.track_record_note: Voted YEA on s4465-119 (FISA surveillance extension) and NAY on hconres75-119 (Iran war powers resolution), consistent with hawkish foreign policy positioning.

top_stances.summary: Bipartisan solution with legal status pathway and stricter border enforcement.
top_stances.source_excerpt: Cosponsored DIGNIDAD Act proposing pathway to legal status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants paired with stricter border enforcement; supported Trump's travel ban.

top_stances.summary: Oppose federal greenhouse gas regulation due to economic concerns.
top_stances.source_excerpt: Skeptical of global warming as basis for Congressional action, fearing economic harm.

top_stances.summary: Oppose gun-control legislation as ineffective for public safety.
top_stances.source_excerpt: Stated after Parkland shooting that gun control would not be effective at stopping mass shootings.

top_stances.summary: Oppose mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenders.
top_stances.source_excerpt: Opposes mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenders.

top_stances.summary: Oppose federal education standards; support state autonomy.
top_stances.source_excerpt: Opposes requiring states to adopt federal education standards.
